Expand the $$\left( x + h \right)^{2}$$ term:
$$\frac{x^{2} + 2 x h + h^{2} - x^{2}}{x^{2} \left( x + h \right)^{2}} = \frac{2 x h + h^{2}}{x^{2} \left( x + h \right)^{2}}$$

To simplify the complex fraction, multiply the numerator by the reciprocal of the denominator ($$1/h$$):

Factor out an $$h$$ from the numerator:
$$f^{\second} ( x ) = \underset{h \rightarrow 0}{\text{lim}} \frac{h \left( 2 x + h \right)}{h \cdot x^{2} \left( x + h \right)^{2}}$$

Cancel the $$h$$ from the numerator and the denominator:
$$f^{\second} ( x ) = \underset{h \rightarrow 0}{\text{lim}} \frac{2 x + h}{x^{2} \left( x + h \right)^{2}}$$

$$f^{\second} ( x ) = \frac{2}{x^{3}}$$
